info.title: Whats Good Customer API
info.description:
REST API for a city/customer to manage their own event pipeline: register **sources** to scrape, review incoming **events**, approve the good ones, and read back the approved feed.

## Authentication
Send your key in the **`X-API-Key`** header (or `?api_key=` query param). Your key is scoped to one customer — you only ever see/modify your own data (another tenant's record returns 404; passing another `customer_id` returns 403).

## Workflow
1. `GET /me` — confirm which city the key is wired to
2. `POST /sources` — register a source (auto-runs a scrape)
3. `GET /events?status=new` — review what was scraped
4. `POST /events/approve` — approve a batch (or `/events/{id}/approve`)
5. `GET /events?status=approved&upcoming=true` — the live feed

Events flow `new → approved | rejected`. Approval is in place — the event stays in this database and flips to `approved`.

get.description: List all sources with optional filters (tenant-scoped for customer keys).

post.description:
Create a new source and automatically run it.

Customer keys always create under their own customer; admins must supply a
``customer_id`` (the column is NOT NULL).

get.description: Get a single source by ID (tenant-scoped).

delete.description: Delete a source. By default keeps events (sets source_id = NULL). Pass clear_events=true to delete associated events.

parameters.name: clear_events

schema.description: Delete associated events instead of nulling source_id
schema.title: Clear Events

parameters.description: Delete associated events instead of nulling source_id

post.description: Run the scraper for a specific source (background task). Tenant-scoped.

get.description: Get the log history for a specific source (tenant-scoped).

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of logs to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of logs to return

get.description: Get a single log entry for a source (tenant-scoped).

post.description:
Run a 1-off duplicate scan for a specific customer and return full results.

Runs two checks against all non-duplicate events for the customer:

1. **Internal**: compares every event against every other event in the local
   database to find pairs that are likely the same event.
2. **Production**: compares every event against the customer's production
   database (via the external API) to find events that may already be live.

If `apply=true`, internal duplicates are written to the database:
`duplicate_of_id` is set and `enhancement_status` is set to `'duplicate'`.
Production matches are reported only (they reference an external ID).

parameters.name: customer_id

schema.description: Customer UUID to scan for duplicates
schema.title: Customer Id

parameters.description: Customer UUID to scan for duplicates

parameters.name: date_proximity_hours

schema.description: Hours within which dates are considered close
schema.title: Date Proximity Hours

parameters.description: Hours within which dates are considered close

parameters.name: apply

schema.description: Mark found duplicates in the database
schema.title: Apply

parameters.description: Mark found duplicates in the database

post.description:
Manually create a single event (tenant-scoped).

Customer keys create under their own customer; admins must supply a
``customer_id``. Written through the canonical ``save_events`` path
(``source_id`` NULL, a generated ``external_id``) so both unique constraints
are respected. The event enters as pending — review and set
``status='approved'`` later via ``PUT /events/{id}``.

get.description:
List events with pagination and filters.

Customer-scoped: a customer key only ever sees its own events regardless of
the ``customer_id`` param. Use ``upcoming=true`` for future events and
``status=approved`` for approved-only (combine for future approved events).

parameters.name: upcoming

schema.description: Only events starting now or in the future
schema.title: Upcoming

parameters.description: Only events starting now or in the future

post.description:
Approve many events at once (status -> 'approved'). Tenant-scoped.

In-place approval for API-only customers: rows stay in this database and
move to 'approved' (no separate production DB). Only events the caller owns
are approved; unknown or other-tenant IDs come back in ``not_found``.

get.description: Get event statistics (scoped to the caller's customer for customer keys).
Get a single event with full details (tenant-scoped).

post.description: Mark an event as a duplicate of another (tenant-scoped).

Approve an event (status -> 'approved'). Tenant-scoped.

In-place approval for API-only customers: the row stays in this database and
moves to 'approved' (no separate production DB / publish step). Consume
approved events via ``GET /events?status=approved``.

get.description:
Return categories used by events matching the given filters.

Tenant-scoped: a customer key only ever sees categories used by its own
events, regardless of the ``customer_id`` param.
Return the authenticated customer's identity and key-relevant config.
Get the status of a background task.

ApiKeyListItem.title: ApiKeyListItem
ApiKeyListItem.description: A key as shown in the admin list (never includes the secret).

ApiKeyUsageResponse.title: ApiKeyUsageResponse
ApiKeyUsageResponse.description: Usage detail for a single key: daily totals + most recent requests.

mime.title: Mime
mime.description: MIME type, e.g. image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp

Attachment.title: Attachment
Attachment.description: A single image attachment for a chat turn (single-turn only — not persisted into context).

BulkApproveRequest.title: BulkApproveRequest
BulkApproveRequest.description: Request to approve many events at once.

BulkApproveResponse.title: BulkApproveResponse
BulkApproveResponse.description:
Result of a bulk approve.

``approved`` lists the IDs moved to 'approved'; ``not_found`` lists IDs that
don't exist or belong to another tenant (existence isn't leaked).

message.title: Message
message.description: Natural language message to process. May be empty when an attachment is provided.

conversation_id.title: Conversation Id
conversation_id.description: ID of existing conversation to continue. Omit to start a new conversation.

context.title: Context
context.description: Additional context to include in the request

attachments.title: Attachments
attachments.description: Image attachments for this turn only. Used by oneoff_screenshot_scrape.

examples.message: Add Green Bench Brewing as a venue

message.title: Message
message.description: Natural language response from the assistant

function_called.description: Details of the function call if one was executed

examples.follow_up_suggestion: Would you like me to run the first scrape?
examples.message: I've added @greenbenchbrewing as an Instagram source. Would you like me to run the first scrape to fetch their events?

customer_id.title: Customer Id
customer_id.description: Customer UUID the key is scoped to. Omit only for an admin key (role='admin').

name.title: Name
name.description: Human label for the key, e.g. 'Asheville onboarding'.

created_by.title: Created By
created_by.description: Who created this key (email/name), for audit.

CreateApiKeyRequest.title: CreateApiKeyRequest
CreateApiKeyRequest.description: Request to issue a new API key.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: The full secret key. Shown ONCE — not retrievable later.

CreateApiKeyResponse.title: CreateApiKeyResponse
CreateApiKeyResponse.description: Returned once on creation. ``api_key`` is shown only here — store it now.

CreateCustomerRequest.title: CreateCustomerRequest
CreateCustomerRequest.description: Request to create a new customer (city).

tenant_config_created.title: Tenant Config Created
tenant_config_created.description: Whether a minimal tenant_config row was created (best-effort).

DuplicatePair.title: DuplicatePair
DuplicatePair.description: An internal duplicate pair found during scanning.

source.title: Source
source.description: Origin tag for the event; defaults to 'manual'.

EventCreate.title: EventCreate
EventCreate.description:
Schema for manually creating a single event via the API.

Tenant and enhancement fields are intentionally not customer-settable:
``customer_id`` is honored only for admin (master) keys — customer keys
always create under their own tenant — and status / category_id / audience_id
/ priority_score are omitted so a manual event enters the normal pending →
enhancement → approval flow, just like a scraped one. Approve later via PUT.

EventUpdate.title: EventUpdate
EventUpdate.description: Schema for updating an event (manual corrections).

function_name.title: Function Name
function_name.description: Name of the function that was called

result.title: Result
result.description: Result data from the function execution

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if the function failed

FunctionCallResult.title: FunctionCallResult
FunctionCallResult.description: Result of a function call executed by the chat system.

event_ids.title: Event Ids
event_ids.description: Ordered list of DB event IDs. Use this OR events, not both.

events.title: Events
events.description: Inline event data. Use this OR event_ids, not both.

MarkDuplicateRequest.title: MarkDuplicateRequest
MarkDuplicateRequest.description: Request to mark event as duplicate.

ProductionDuplicatePair.title: ProductionDuplicatePair
ProductionDuplicatePair.description: A match between a local event and a production event.

ScraperLogListResponse.title: ScraperLogListResponse
ScraperLogListResponse.description: Response schema for paginated list of scraper logs.

ScraperLogResponse.title: ScraperLogResponse
ScraperLogResponse.description: Response schema for scraper log entries.

cover_headline.title: Cover Headline
cover_headline.description: Override for the cover slide headline. Defaults to 'Top Events This Weekend in {city}'.

include_caption.title: Include Caption
include_caption.description: Whether to generate an Instagram caption via AI.

template_name.title: Template Name
template_name.description: Template to use. Overrides the tenant's configured template.

cover_image_url.title: Cover Image Url
cover_image_url.description: Background image URL for cover/end slides. Falls back to DB config or first event image.

logo_url.title: Logo Url
logo_url.description: Logo image URL. Falls back to DB config.

allow_venue_creation.title: Allow Venue Creation
allow_venue_creation.description: Whether the customer is allowed to create venues via chat / admin UI.

create_if_found.title: Create If Found
create_if_found.description: If True, create the venue in database if found via search

VenueSearchRequest.title: VenueSearchRequest
VenueSearchRequest.description: Request for venue search via SerpAPI.
